title: did:bolyra DID Method Specification with VC issuance

artifacts.description: W3C DID Method specification for did:bolyra, including method syntax, DID Document structure, CRUD operations, security considerations, and privacy considerations per W3C DID Core 1.0
JSON-LD Verifiable Credential schema for handshake results, defining credentialSubject fields: scopeCommitment, sessionNonce, verifierDID, humanityProofRoot, agentCapabilityRoot

Cryptographic suite specification for BolyraVerification2024 verification method type, defining key material representation (BN254 elliptic curve points), proof purpose, and DID Document embedding rules
On-chain DID Document registry anchoring service endpoints and key rotations; wraps IdentityRegistry with DID-resolution-friendly view functions keyed by identityCommitment
DID resolver implementing the did:bolyra resolution algorithm: decode DID → derive identityCommitment → query humanTree/agentTree membership → construct and return DID Document JSON-LD
VC issuance module: takes a completed handshake result, signs a BolyraHandshakeCredential as the verifier service DID, and returns a signed JWT-VC or JSON-LD Verifiable Credential
DID Document builder: constructs conformant DID Documents including verificationMethod, authentication, assertionMethod, and service endpoint sections from on-chain state
Unit and integration tests for the DID resolver: valid resolution, deactivated identity, non-member identity, malformed DID, and chain query failure paths
Tests for VC issuance and verification: schema conformance, signature verification, credential expiry, revocation check, and round-trip JWT-VC parse
Interoperability tests against did-resolver universal resolver interface and vc-js / @digitalbazaar/vc libraries to validate W3C ecosystem compatibility
W3C DID Specification Registries submission template and instructions for registering did:bolyra, including method-specific parameters and contact information

steps: Define the did:bolyra method syntax: did:bolyra:<base58btc-encoded-identity-commitment> (32-byte BN254 field element encoded as base58btc, no version or network prefix in v1)

Specify DID Document JSON-LD structure: @context includes W3C DID Core + bolyra-specific context; id = the DID; verificationMethod array with one BolyraVerification2024 entry containing the BN254 public key; authentication and assertionMethod reference the verification method; service array includes IdentityRegistry contract address as a LinkedDomains or custom type endpoint

Define BolyraVerification2024 cryptographic suite: key type = BN254G1 (matches Semaphore/Poseidon stack); publicKeyMultibase encodes the identity commitment; proof purpose mapping to Semaphore group membership proofs

Specify resolution algorithm: (1) parse DID, extract base58-encoded string, base58btc-decode to 32-byte identity commitment; (2) call IdentityRegistry.isMember(commitment, humanTreeId) or agentTree equivalent to verify membership and retrieve group root; (3) fetch any key rotation or service endpoint deltas from BolyraDidRegistry; (4) assemble DID Document and return with metadata including deactivated flag if commitment is no longer in tree

Specify CRUD operations: Create = calling IdentityRegistry.registerIdentity() constitutes DID creation, no separate on-chain DID create tx needed; Update = key rotation via BolyraDidRegistry.setServiceEndpoint(); Delete/Deactivate = calling IdentityRegistry.revokeIdentity(); Read = resolution algorithm above

Design BolyraHandshakeCredential VC schema: type = ['VerifiableCredential', 'BolyraHandshakeCredential']; issuer = did:bolyra:<verifier-service-identity-commitment>; credentialSubject.id = did:bolyra:<prover-identity-commitment>; credentialSubject.scopeCommitment = hex string; credentialSubject.sessionNonce = hex string; credentialSubject.verifiedAt = ISO8601 timestamp; credentialSubject.humanityAsserted = bool; expirationDate = sessionNonce-derived TTL

Implement BolyraDidRegistry contract: mapping(uint256 => ServiceEndpoint[]) for identity-commitment-keyed endpoints; event-based key rotation log; view function resolveDocument(uint256 commitment) returns packed DID Document fields; integrate with existing IdentityRegistry for membership checks

Implement SDK resolver.ts: conform to did-resolver DIDResolver interface (resolve(did, options) => DIDResolutionResult); handle did:bolyra method prefix guard; implement base58btc decode; call on-chain view functions via ethers.js provider; construct JSON-LD DID Document; set didDocumentMetadata.deactivated correctly

Implement SDK issuer.ts: accept HandshakeResult struct from existing protocol; build VC payload per schema; sign using verifier service's private key via did-jwt or jsonld-signatures; support both JWT-VC compact serialization and JSON-LD Linked Data Proofs output formats
Register bolyra-specific JSON-LD context at a stable URL (or inline in spec): define BolyraVerification2024, BolyraHandshakeCredential, scopeCommitment, sessionNonce, humanityAsserted terms

Write resolver tests covering: happy-path human identity, happy-path agent identity, deactivated identity returns deactivated=true, identity not in any tree returns notFound error, malformed base58 DID returns invalidDid error

Write VC issuance tests covering: credential schema validation with ajv, JWT-VC round-trip with did-jwt, JSON-LD proof round-trip with @digitalbazaar/vc, expired credential rejection, tampered credentialSubject rejection

Write interop tests: resolve did:bolyra DID using did-resolver universal resolver shim, verify BolyraHandshakeCredential using vc-js against published context, confirm W3C VC Data Model 2.0 conformance checklist

Draft did:bolyra method specification document following W3C DID Method specification template: abstract, terminology, method name, method-specific identifier, DID Document, operations, security considerations (replay attacks via sessionNonce, Sybil resistance via Semaphore group membership, key compromise recovery), privacy considerations (commitment unlinkability, selective disclosure via ZKP)
Prepare W3C DID Specification Registries submission: fill method name, specification URL, contact, implementation status, and test suite URL fields
dependencies: IdentityRegistry contract (humanTree and agentTree membership storage and query interface)
Semaphore group Merkle tree (BN254 / Poseidon hash — determines identity commitment derivation and key type)
Handshake protocol session result struct (scopeCommitment, sessionNonce fields must be finalized before VC schema locks)
W3C Verifiable Credentials Data Model 2.0 specification
did-jwt or @digitalbazaar/vc (VC signing library — choice affects proof format)
base58btc codec (multiformats or bs58 npm package)
JSON-LD context hosting (stable URL required for W3C registry submission)
